assign_high: Sleep in atomic context (e.g., GFP_KERNEL allocation or blocking calls while holding spinlock or in IRQ)
Deadlock risk from double-acquire of same lock or inconsistent lock ordering
User pointer misuse leading to crashes or info leaks (e.g., printk with %s on __user, incorrect copy_from_user semantics causing out-of-bounds or uninitialized reads)
Publishing shared pointers or data for lockless readers without proper memory ordering (missing smp_wmb/WRITE_ONCE on publish, missing READ_ONCE on consume)
RCU read-side critical section containing sleeping/blocking operations
assign_medium: Allocation failure paths not checked or not handled (e.g., GFP_ATOMIC unchecked)
Using vmalloc memory for DMA or DMA mapping without dma_mapping_error checks
Module init error path does not unwind previously initialized resources (missing goto-based reverse cleanup)
assign_low: Misunderstanding of mutex_trylock return semantics (1=success, 0=busy) with limited impact
Minor style or robustness issues that do not cause immediate failure but reduce reliability

Atomic Context: no blocking calls inside RCU read-side
Allocation Failures: GFP_ATOMIC can return NULL; check and handle
vmalloc memory is not suitable for DMA
prefer kzalloc to avoid info leaks
User Pointer Handling: copy_from_user returns bytes not copied; 0 means success
copy user memory to kernel buffer and validate before use
Memory Ordering: use WRITE_ONCE with smp_wmb before publishing
spinlock release provides barriers but lockless paths need explicit ordering
Module Error Paths: on init failure, undo prior steps in reverse order
only free/unregister what was actually initialized
Locking Mistakes: avoid double-acquisition of the same lock
maintain consistent lock ordering across code paths
